# Anisotropic flow generated by hard partons in medium

> Hard partons which are produced copiously in nuclear collisions at the LHC, deposit most of their energy and momentum into the surrounding quark-gluon plasma. We show that this generates streams in the plasma and contributes importantly to flow anisotropies. With the help of event-by-event three-dimensional perfect hydrodynamic simulations we calculate the observable azimuthal anisotropies of hadronic distributions and show that the proposed mechanism is capable of generating non-negligible part of the observed signal. Hence, it must be taken into account in quantitative studies in which one tries to extract the values of viscosities from the comparison of simulated results with measured data.

# Anisotropic flow generated by hard partons in medium

## Key claim

Hard partons produced in nuclear collisions at the LHC deposit energy and momentum into the quark-gluon plasma, generating streams that contribute non-negligibly to azimuthal flow anisotropies; this mechanism must be accounted for in quantitative studies extracting viscosity values from comparisons of simulated results with measured data, though the authors note that the effect is shown via perfect-fluid simulations and that viscous simulations are planned for the future.

## Method

The authors performed event-by-event three-dimensional perfect hydrodynamic simulations using the SHASTA algorithm, with initial energy density profiles from the optical Glauber model for Pb-Pb collisions at sqrt(s_NN) = 5.5 TeV. Hard parton positions followed the distribution of binary nucleon-nucleon collisions, and energy-momentum deposition into the plasma was modeled via a force term with Gaussian spatial smearing (sigma = 0.3 fm), where the energy loss per unit length scaled with entropy density. Hadronic observables were generated using THERMINATOR2 from the freeze-out hypersurfaces.

## Key numbers

- Impact parameter for non-central collisions: b = 6.5 fm, corresponding to 30-40% centrality class
- Gaussian width for energy-momentum deposition from hard partons: sigma_i = 0.3 fm
- Reference entropy density s_0 corresponds to energy density of 20 GeV/fm^3
- 100 central events were simulated with included hard partons, each run through THERMINATOR2 five times
- Collision energy: sqrt(s_NN) = 5.5 TeV (full LHC energy for Pb nuclei)

## Context

This work extends earlier studies of jet-induced flow, such as Tomasik (2011) which indicated that such a mechanism would generate elliptic flow, and contrasts with Tachibana (year not given) who studied only single dijets (which cannot produce alignment with collision geometry), Noronha (year not given) who used 2D boost-invariant simulations (inappropriate for this symmetry-breaking mechanism), and Florchinger and Zapp (years not given) who studied only radial flow. The results enable more precise extraction of transport coefficients from flow data, provided future work includes three-dimensional viscous hydrodynamics and other fluctuation sources.
